# Omics Source-First Density Fixture

## Course Knowledge Map
The notes are organised by knowledge blocks from the source material.

### Module: Omics vocabulary
Priority: ★★
Exam Specificity:
Definition.
Core Exam Claim:
Omics vocabulary sets the scale and type of biological data being interpreted.

### Module: PCR primer logic
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
PCR primer logic defines the amplified region, specificity, and orientation of amplification.

### Module: DNA ligase mechanism
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Mechanism.
Core Exam Claim:
DNA ligase mechanism joins compatible DNA ends by forming a phosphodiester bond.

### Module: Restriction cloning
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Restriction cloning uses restriction enzyme digestion and compatible ends to insert DNA into a vector.

### Module: BioBrick Golden Gate Gibson cloning
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Comparison.
Core Exam Claim:
BioBrick, Golden Gate, and Gibson cloning are separate assembly strategies with different end-joining logic.

### Module: Mutagenesis QuikChange DpnI
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Mutagenesis by QuikChange uses primers to introduce a change and DpnI to remove methylated template DNA.

### Module: Sanger sequencing
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Sanger sequencing uses chain-terminating nucleotides to infer sequence from fragment termination.

### Module: 454 pyrosequencing
Priority: ★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
454 pyrosequencing links nucleotide incorporation to a pyrophosphate-dependent light signal.

### Module: Illumina platform choice
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Illumina platform choice depends on sequencing-by-synthesis readout, throughput, read length, and experiment aim.

### Module: Coverage and assembly
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Graph interpretation.
Core Exam Claim:
Coverage and assembly connect read depth, overlap, and confidence in reconstructed sequence.

### Module: BLAST homology orthology paralogy
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Definition.
Core Exam Claim:
BLAST supports similarity searching, while homology, orthology, and paralogy describe evolutionary relationships.

### Module: Genome annotation pipeline
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Genome annotation pipeline logic moves from feature prediction to functional assignment and validation.

### Module: Transcriptomics RNA-seq
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Transcriptomics RNA-seq measures RNA abundance and supports differential expression analysis.

### Module: Protein informatics
Priority: ★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Protein informatics uses sequence and structural features to infer protein properties and possible function.

### Module: Expression systems
Priority: ★★
Exam Specificity:
Criteria list.
Core Exam Claim:
Expression systems are chosen by matching host biology, protein requirements, yield, cost, and downstream use.

### Module: His-tag purification
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
His-tag purification uses affinity binding to capture tagged proteins and elution to recover enriched protein.

### Module: Proteomics workflow
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Proteomics workflow links protein separation, mass spectrometry, database matching, and biological interpretation.

### Module: Metabolomics
Priority: ★★
Exam Specificity:
Method workflow.
Core Exam Claim:
Metabolomics profiles small molecules to infer biochemical state and pathway changes.

### Module: Genetic manipulation CRISPR homologous recombination
Priority: ★★★
Exam Specificity:
Comparison.
Core Exam Claim:
Genetic manipulation by CRISPR and homologous recombination changes genetic information through distinct targeting and repair logic.
